home.headline: Design your Kanban system
home.intro: Start from a template or a blank board, then refine columns, WIP limits, and swim lanes.
home.wipText: Constraining work in progress exposes bottlenecks, shortens cycle time, and improves predictability — start finishing, stop starting.
home.noBoards: No saved boards yet. Create one from the Templates tab.
home.delete_confirm: Delete this board from this device?
home.pick_or_create: Choose a board from Home, create a blank board, or pick a template.
principlesList: Visualize the workflow — make invisible work visible.
Limit WIP — start finishing, stop starting.
Implement feedback loops — improve the system.

designer.no_columns: Add a column to get started, or pick a template.

templates.title: Board Templates
templates.subtitle: Choose a pre-built board archetype as a starting point

context.basic: Best for teams just starting with Kanban. Simple, low-overhead. Grow from here.
context.development: For software teams with code review and QA stages. Good starting point for engineering squads.
context.support: Service desk with priority swim lanes (Critical / Normal). Fast triage orientation.
context.personal: David Allen-inspired personal productivity system. WIP limit on 'Today' is key.
context.marketing: Content production pipeline with topic swim lanes. Keeps different content types visible.
context.ops: IT and operations change management with emergency lane. Helps separate planned vs reactive work.
context.research: Discovery and research teams. Focus on flow of insights, not just tasks.
context.hiring: Talent acquisition pipeline. Swim lanes per role/department to track parallel pipelines.
context.product: Product discovery flow from opportunity to shipped. Double-diamond inspired.
context.crisis: Incident management with priority lanes. Clear escalation path from detected to post-mortem.

learn.title: Kanban Principles
learn.wip_body: WIP (Work In Progress) limits constrain how many items can be in a column at once. Little's Law: Throughput = WIP / Cycle Time. Reducing WIP reduces cycle time and exposes bottlenecks faster.
learn.flow_body: Flow efficiency = active time / total time. Most work items spend >70% of their life waiting, not being worked on. Visualizing flow (via Kanban) is the first step to improving it.
learn.p1: Visualize the workflow — make invisible work visible.
learn.p2: Limit WIP — start finishing, stop starting.
learn.p3: Manage flow — measure and optimize cycle time.
learn.p4: Make process policies explicit — clear rules reduce conflict.
learn.p5: Improve collaboratively — use models to guide change.
